data.title: Wall Street Hits Fresh Highs As Ceasefire Hopes Lift Sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street equities closed at fresh record highs, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq extending their gains as investor sentiment improved on signs of easing tensions in the Middle East.

The Nasdaq Composite rose 0.36% to 24,102.70, marking its 12th straight day of gains, its longest winning streak since July 2009. The S&P 500 added 0.26% to 7,041.28 while the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.24% to 48,578.72. Both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also touched intraday record levels.

Markets found support after US President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon had agreed to a 10-day ceasefire, while also signalling a possible meeting between the US and Iran over the weekend. Earlier reports indicated that any broader agreement with Iran could take several months, keeping trading conditions volatile.

“You’ve got markets fluctuating between more positive and slightly neutral headlines,” said Chris Zaccarelli, chief investment officer at Northlight Asset Management. “For the last month and a half, trading has all been about the Iran war.”

Despite the positive momentum, analysts said the rally may require clearer signs of sustained diplomatic progress to continue. Economic data released during the session showed mixed signals, with US jobless claims falling more than expected, suggesting a stable labour market even as employers remain cautious.

“The war is still the single most important driver of the market,” said Robert Phipps, a director at Per Stirling Capital Management. “The rubber band was very stretched to the downside. It has snapped back and is no longer stretched to the downside … Now the market needs to start trading on its own fundamentals.”

Sector performance was broadly positive, with energy stocks leading gains as oil prices rose, while healthcare lagged. Corporate earnings also shaped sentiment, with PepsiCo rising after beating profit estimates, while Abbott Laboratories fell after lowering its full-year outlook.

Among notable movers, Myseum surged 129% after rebranding to focus on artificial intelligence, highlighting continued speculative interest in AI-related plays.

Market breadth remained positive, with advancing stocks outnumbering decliners on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, as trading volume came in slightly below the recent average.

Reuters

data.title: SGX Open Slightly Lower As Regional Sentiment Tracks Wall Street Record Highs
data.description:
Singapore equities opened marginally lower, with the Straits Times Index (STI) slipping 0.15% to 5,000.16 at 9.09 am, as investors balanced Wall Street’s record-setting rally against cautious risk sentiment in Asian trading.

The STI was down 7.67 points in early trade, with mixed breadth across the Singapore market. On the broader board, 120 counters advanced against 97 decliners, suggesting selective buying despite the index weakness. Early trading volume stood at S$227.56 million across 162.56 million shares.

Regional cues remained supportive after US equities closed at fresh highs overnight, driven by easing geopolitical tensions and expectations of diplomatic progress in the Middle East. The S&P 500 rose 0.26% while the Nasdaq added 0.36%, extending its winning streak to 12 straight sessions, the longest since 2009.

Sentiment was lifted after reports of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon and remarks from US President Donald Trump indicating potential talks with Iran over the weekend. Market participants also weighed mixed US economic data, including a larger-than-expected drop in jobless claims, which pointed to labour market resilience.

In Singapore, index heavyweights were mostly subdued in early trade. DBS slipped 0.09% to S$57.25 while SGX edged down to S$21.08. UOB was flat at S$37.45, while OCBC traded at S$22.67. Singtel held at S$4.81, and Keppel eased to S$11.92.

In derivatives, activity remained muted in early trade across key contracts, with MSCI Singapore Index Futures last at 452.55 and FTSE China A50 Index Futures at 15,442.

data.title: Bitcoin Stalls Below US$75,000 As Ethereum Slips
data.description:
Bitcoin hovered just shy of the key US$75,000 threshold on April 17, caught in a tight consolidation band as investors weighed macro risks against steady institutional inflows.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency has been “coiling” near resistance, with price action reflecting indecision after a recent push toward the psychological ceiling.

While Bitcoin held its ground, Ethereum edged lower in recent sessions, underperforming its larger peer and reinforcing a growing divergence between the two dominant digital assets.

However, the broader picture suggests a market pausing rather than reversing. Bitcoin’s climb from roughly US$71,000 levels earlier in the week to the mid-US$74,000 range highlights underlying demand, supported in part by continued inflows into spot ETFs.

Yet momentum is fragile as analysts point to US$75,000 as a critical breakout level, one that has repeatedly capped rallies. Failure to decisively clear it could keep prices range-bound in the near term, while a confirmed breakout may open the path toward higher targets.

Ethereum’s softer tone, meanwhile, adds another layer of caution. Despite earlier rebounds this month, the asset has struggled to sustain upward momentum, reflecting weaker investor conviction relative to Bitcoin and ongoing underperformance in 2026.

Macro forces remain the dominant driver. Easing geopolitical tensions and risk-on sentiment in equities have lent support to crypto, but uncertainty still clouds directional conviction.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Inches Higher At Midday On Hopes Of West Asia De-Escalation
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia traded firmer at midday, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rising 0.23% as improving sentiment over a potential easing of tensions in West Asia lifted risk appetite.

At 12:30pm, the FBM KLCI added 3.85 points to 1,687.27, after moving between 1,682.81 and an intraday high of 1,690.83.

Broader market indicators were also positive, with the FBM 70 climbing 120.96 points (0.69%) to 17,624.23, while the FBM Emas and FBM Shariah indices gained 45.27 points (0.36%) to 12,454.53 and 55.42 points (0.45%) to 12,387.49, respectively, signalling a broad-based recovery across sectors.

Market breadth, meanwhile, remained healthy, with gainers outpacing losers 628 to 455, while 491 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,157 stocks were untraded and 23 suspended.

Among actively traded counters, Borneo Oil Bhd surged to dominate volumes with close to 179.6 million shares, while Zetrix AI Bhd and MMAG Holdings Bhd also saw strong interest. Tech-linked Inari Amertron Bhd advanced notably at 12 sen with around 39.2 million shares changing hand, reflecting renewed buying in semiconductor plays.

On the leaderboard, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led gainers, followed by UMS Integration Ltd and plantation player United Plantations Bhd, while banking heavyweight Hong Leong Bank Bhd also edged higher.

Decliners were led by Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd and PPB Group Bhd, alongside Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd, as select commodity-linked counters saw mild profit-taking.

Overall, the trading activity was robust, with 2.3 billion shares changing hands valued at RM1.63 billion.

The cautious optimism reflects a market still sensitive to geopolitical developments, but increasingly willing to price in a stabilisation scenario after recent volatility.

data.title: Breakout Lifts HSIF, RHB Flags Resistance Risk At 26,600
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd maintained its negative trading stance on Hang Seng Index Futures despite a technical breakout above the 26,000-point level, citing firm resistance at 26,600 points.

The research house said HSIF extended its counter-trend rebound on Thursday, rising 371 points to close at 26,351 after opening at 25,971. The index rebounded from an intraday low of 25,885 to hit a high of 26,420 before forming a bullish candlestick, signalling short-term upward momentum.

However, it noted that the index has yet to close above the key 26,600-point resistance, which remains intact. In evening trade, HSIF slipped 122 points to 26,229, reflecting lingering caution in the market.

RHB said while the breakout above 26,000 could prompt a further attempt to challenge 26,600, the level is expected to hold in the current bearish setup unless decisively breached.

“Although it underwent a positive price action, the index has yet to close above the 26,600-point resistance,” the research house said, adding that a sustained move above this level would improve overall market sentiment.

For now, RHB recommended maintaining short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600.

On the downside, immediate support is seen at 25,100, followed by 24,500, while resistance levels are pegged at 26,600 and 27,200.

The research house said the broader outlook remains cautious, with the prevailing trend still tilted to the downside unless key resistance levels are convincingly broken.

data.title: Stock Today: Uzma Rises 1.25% As Petronas Contract Wins Lift Upstream Growth Visibility
data.description:
Uzma Bhd traded higher in afternoon trade after securing fresh Petronas Carigali contracts that analysts say reinforce earnings visibility and signal stronger activity in Malaysia’s upstream oil and gas sector.

As at the time of writing, the stock was up 1.25% or 0.5 sen to RM0.405, trading between RM0.400 and RM0.410 with volume of 16.79 million shares, compared with an opening price of RM0.400. The counter has largely hovered within a tight range in recent sessions, but sentiment turned slightly positive following the latest contract announcements.

The gains come after Uzma announced that its subsidiary Setegap Ventures Petroleum has secured three contracts from Petronas Carigali for coiled tubing unit equipment and services across Sabah and Sarawak as well as completion packages in Malaysia. The five-year contracts will run from 13 February 2026 to 12 February 2031 and cover well intervention, completion, idle well reactivation and production enhancement activities.

While contract value was not disclosed, Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga Research) estimated similar jobs typically average around RM100 million annually, suggesting potential total value of about RM500 million or higher depending on execution scope. The research house also noted that Uzma’s year-to-date wins now stand at about RM800 million, representing around 89% of its full-year replenishment forecast.

Kenanga Research maintained its positive stance on Uzma with an unchanged target price of RM0.70, saying the latest award was within expectations and would be progressively recognised over the contract period. It added that the contracts highlight continued brownfield activity as operators focus on boosting output from mature assets amid energy security concerns.

The research house also pointed to Uzma’s expansion into sustainable energy and digitalisation segments as longer-term catalysts, although contributions remain limited for now. However, it cautioned that risks include oil price volatility, execution challenges and rising costs.

data.title: Stock Picks: F&N And Oriental Kopi
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd (F&N) and Oriental Kopi Holdings Bhd as near-term trading opportunities, citing bullish technical breakouts supported by improving price momentum and volume trends.

The research house said F&N has resumed its uptrend after breaking above the RM30.50 resistance level, supported by a bullish candlestick formation and a move above its 21-day simple moving average.

It noted that the breakout signals a positive near-term trend, with the stock potentially advancing towards RM32.50 and subsequently RM34 if momentum is sustained. However, it cautioned that a decline below the RM29 support level could invalidate the bullish setup.

Meanwhile, RHB Research highlighted Oriental Kopi as another counter showing strong upward momentum after surging past the RM1.10 resistance on rising trading volume. The research house said the increase in volume alongside price gains indicates strengthening buying interest, reinforcing the bullish outlook.

It expects the stock to test the next resistance levels at RM1.20 and RM1.30 in the near term, while noting that a drop below the RM1.00 level may trigger a corrective pullback.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Edges Higher At Midday On Upbeat GDP Signal, But Breadth Stays Weak
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia clawed into positive territory by midday on April 17, buoyed by stronger-than-expected economic signals, though underlying sentiment remained cautious with decliners still outpacing gainers.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 1.53 points, or 0.09%, to 1,691.24, reversing earlier losses after the Department of Statistics Malaysia projected the economy to expand 5.3% in the first quarter of 2026.

The modest rebound in the benchmark index was mirrored across broader indices, with FBM 70, FBM Emas and FBM Shariah gauges also inching higher, suggesting selective buying interest despite a lack of strong conviction.

Trading activity was brisk, with 1.88 billion shares changing hands worth RM1.4 billion, as retail-driven counters dominated the most active list.

Among them, Empire Premium Food Bhd surged 36 sen to RM1.06, emerging as both the top active and one of the top gainers, while Borneo Oil Bhd and Zetrix AI Bhd also saw heavy trading volumes.

On the blue-chip front, United Plantations Bhd led gainers, climbing 72 sen to RM34.28, followed by Allianz Malaysia Bhd and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, reflecting pockets of strength in defensive and consumer-linked stocks.

However, losses in selected heavyweights capped broader upside. Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd fell 30 sen to RM33.68, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd and Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd also retreated.

Despite the headline gains, market breadth painted a more fragile picture, with 471 losers outnumbering 427 gainers, underscoring lingering caution among investors.

Overall, the stronger GDP outlook offered a timely lift to Bursa’s benchmark, but with selling still broad-based, the market’s midday rebound looks more like a tentative bounce than a decisive shift in sentiment.

data.title: Bursa Opens Lower As Blue Chips Slip, Tracking Softer Oil And Mixed Wall Street Tone
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia started Friday’s session in the red, with the FBM KLCI edging lower in early trade as weakness in heavyweight counters and softer commodity cues weighed on sentiment.

As at 9.32am, the FBM KLCI stood at 1,684.51, down 5.20 points or 0.31%. The index moved between 1,682.88 and 1,689.07 in early trading.

Broader market indices also trended lower. The FBM70 fell 47.50 points or 0.27% to 17,594.17, while the FBM EMAS slipped 36.84 points or 0.30% to 12,434.78. The FBM Shariah Index declined 43.56 points or 0.35% to 12,363.28, while the FBM Emas Shariah Index eased 3.20 points or 0.32% to 1,002.87.

Sentiment was partly influenced by softer crude oil prices, which retreated in early Asian trade as easing geopolitical tensions and renewed hopes of US-Iran talks reduced concerns over supply disruptions. The move came after a 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel took effect, cooling recent risk premiums in energy markets.

Global cues were also mixed after Wall Street closed at fresh record highs overnight, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq extending gains on optimism over easing Middle East tensions. However, analysts noted that momentum remains sensitive to shifting headlines, with investors still focused on geopolitical developments and economic signals.

Locally, market breadth was uneven in early trade, with selective buying in defensive and consumer names offset by selling pressure in index-heavy sectors.

Among index constituents, banks were slightly softer, with DBS edging lower while OCBC Bank and UOB traded with limited movement. Singtel and Keppel were broadly steady.

Empire Premium Food Bhd remained in focus, continuing its post-IPO momentum with active trading as it held well above its listing price. The counter was among the most heavily traded stocks in early session.

Other active counters included Zetrix, Toyoven and Armada, which saw mixed price action across small and mid-cap segments.

data.title: Stock Today: Tuju Setia Jumps 13% Amid Securing RM359 Million Ara Damansara Residential Contract
data.description:
Tuju Setia Bhd surged in late trading after the construction group secured a RM359.3 million residential contract from Sime Darby Property yesterday, lifting its order book to a record high and strengthening earnings visibility through 2029.

As at the time of writing, the stock was up 13.16% or 2.5 sen to RM0.215, after opening at RM0.200 and trading between RM0.200 and RM0.215. Volume stood at 871,000 shares, well above recent sessions, with buying interest concentrated at the RM0.200 to RM0.215 range.

The rally followed Tuju Setia’s announcement that its wholly owned subsidiary Pembinaan Tuju Setia Sdn Bhd has been appointed main contractor for the Ara Bloc Residences project in Ara Damansara. The development involves three serviced apartment blocks with a 9-storey podium housing facilities, parking, retail units, utilities and lobbies.

The contract is valued at RM359.3 million and is scheduled for completion within 40 months, with construction set to commence on 21 April 2026. The latest win marks another repeat mandate from Sime Darby Property, reinforcing Tuju Setia’s positioning in the high-rise residential construction segment.

The group said the new project lifts its outstanding order book to about RM2.4 billion, providing stronger revenue visibility over the medium term as construction works are progressively executed.

Market participants viewed the contract win as a significant boost for sentiment given the scale of the job relative to Tuju Setia’s market capitalisation and recent trading range. The counter has largely traded below RM0.25 over the past year, making the latest contract-driven rally one of its sharpest short-term moves in recent months.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher, Broader Market Stays Positive As Oil Eases On US-Iran Peace Hopes
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened slightly higher, tracking firmer regional sentiment and easing crude oil prices as markets priced in potential progress in US-Iran peace negotiations that could reduce geopolitical risk in the Strait of Hormuz.

The FBMKLCI rose 2.58 points or 0.15% to 1,686.00 in early trade. Broader indices also edged higher, with the FBM70 up 66.68 points or 0.38% to 17,569.95, while the FBMEMAS gained 27.64 points or 0.22% to 12,436.90.

The FBMSHA advanced 36.21 points or 0.29% to 12,368.28, while the F4GBM index added 2.25 points or 0.22% to 1,003.47, reflecting a generally positive tone across the market.

Sentiment was supported by Wall Street’s overnight gains, where US equities hit fresh records as investors grew more optimistic that tensions between Washington and Tehran could ease, helping to stabilise energy markets.

Oil prices eased in early trade after reports suggested Iran could allow freer navigation through parts of the Strait of Hormuz under a potential agreement, easing fears of prolonged supply disruptions in a route that handles around 20% of global oil flows.

On the broader market, advance-decline figures showed a positive bias, with 197 gainers against 111 decliners, suggesting underlying buying interest despite cautious trading at the index level.

Trading volume stood at 297.44 million shares with a value of S$329 million.

Among actively traded counters, MMAG was unchanged at 0.035 with 625,878 lots traded, while GD Group rose 0.025 to 0.475. AAX added 0.020 to 1.260, while Velesto was flat at 0.330.

On the blue-chip front, gains were seen in UMS International, which rose 0.17 or 9.50% to 1.960. Nestlé gained 0.480 to 99.480, Allianz added 0.220 to 21.000 and MBM Resources climbed 0.220 to 5.100.

Decliners included PChem, which fell 0.130 to 5.500, CIMB down 0.080 to 7.420, PetDag easing 0.120 to 20.600, Heineken slipping 0.120 to 23.060 and MPI down 0.160 to 32.840.

In the banking space, DBS traded at 57.320, OCBC at 22.770 and UOB at 37.490, while Singtel was at 4.890. SGX was last seen at 21.050.

Market participants noted that while sentiment has improved on expectations of de-escalation in the Middle East, volatility may persist as investors continue to monitor developments around US-Iran talks and their potential impact on global energy flows and inflation.

data.title: Ringgit Extends Gains Against Dollar, Others
data.description:
The ringgit experienced gains against the US dollar and other major currencies on Friday, supported by strong domestic sentiment, according to Bernama.

Markets are anticipating Malaysia’s advance estimate of the first-quarter 2026 (1Q 2026) gross domestic product (GDP) at noon, which could further stabilize the local currency.

At 8 am, the local currency strengthened to 3.9505/9565 against the greenback from 3.9520/9560 at Thursday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit is expected to hold around RM3.95 today.

“All eyes are on the advance estimate for 1Q 2026 GDP, with median forecasts at 5.5 per cent (4Q 2025: 6.3 per cent). The data could support the ringgit,” he told Bernama.

On the global front, he said optimism over a second round of US–Iran talks and prospects for a permanent ceasefire could lift risk sentiment, supporting emerging market currencies, including the ringgit.

At the open, the ringgit traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

It strengthened against the British pound to 5.3431/3512 from 5.3506/3560 at Thursday’s close, rose versus the euro to 4.6537/6608 from 4.6551/6598, and gained against the Japanese yen to 2.4812/4851 from 2.4848/4874.

The local currency traded mostly higher against its ASEAN peers.

It climbed against the Singapore dollar to 3.1026/1078 from 3.1069/1103 on Thursday, appreciated versus the Thai baht to 12.3222/3502 from 12.3473/3664, and edged up against the Philippine peso to 6.58/6.60 from 6.59/6.60, but was flat against the Indonesian rupiah at 230.5/230.9.

data.title: Stock Today: ITMAX Slips 1% As Investors Lock In Profits After RM603 Million Johor CCTV Win
data.description:
ITMAX System Bhd traded lower in the late morning session after recent contract-driven gains, with the stock easing 0.050 or 1.01% to RM4.890 as investors locked in profits following its sharp rally on the back of a new Johor Bahru surveillance contract announcement.

At 11.57 am, the counter opened at RM4.960 and moved between RM4.870 and RM5.020 before settling near session lows, with turnover recorded at 11,476 lots. Buying interest was thin at 99 lots against selling pressure of 598 lots, while last traded volume weighted price stood at RM4.940.

The weakness came shortly after ITMAX announced it had secured a RM603.5 million variation order from Majlis Bandaraya Johor Bahru for the deployment of additional artificial intelligence driven CCTV systems under a 240 month service contract, reinforcing its position in Malaysia’s smart city surveillance rollout.

The contract involves the installation of 2,000 additional CCTV units on top of the existing 500 units, with deployment expected to take around one year and capital expenditure estimated between RM70 million and RM80 million. The deal is expected to strengthen long term recurring income visibility, with analysts previously flagging earnings contribution to be reflected from 2027 onwards.

Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd maintained a Buy call on the stock with a higher target price of RM6.04, citing stronger earnings visibility, expanding Johor rollout momentum and potential upside from future Kuala Lumpur City Hall and other municipal contracts.

Despite the bullish analyst view, the stock saw mild profit taking after recent strength, with investors assessing near term valuation following the sharp run up linked to growing expectations of wider national smart city contract wins across multiple local councils.

data.title: ITMAX Captures Steady Income Stream
data.description:
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) maintained its BUY call on ITMAX System Bhd with a higher target price of RM6.04 versus RM5.74 previously, citing stronger earnings visibility from expanded CCTV deployment.

The research house highlights upside from Johor rollout momentum, potential Kuala Lumpur City Hall phase expansion and long-term smart city monetisation opportunities.

ITMAX System has secured a Letter of Variation Order through its 65% owned subsidiary Southmax from Majlis Bandaraya Johor Bahru for the deployment of additional artificial intelligence video surveillance systems under a 240-month service subscription model, marking another expansion of its smart city infrastructure footprint.

The latest contract involves the installation of an additional 2,000 CCTV units compared with the existing 500 units, with deployment expected to take around one year and capex estimated at RM70 million to RM80 million. The research house said monthly charges of about RM1,200 per unit are broadly in line with existing benchmarks, adding that earnings contribution is expected to be fully reflected in the second half of 2027.

HLIB estimated the expanded rollout could generate around RM18 million in annual revenue and RM7.5 million in net profit after minority interests, supported by ITMAX’s RM130 million cash position alongside cash flow from its smart parking operations and existing CCTV base.

It added that further upside could emerge from additional variation orders as local councils pursue wider surveillance expansion targets.

The research house said investor focus will gradually shift towards ITMAX’s project pipeline beyond 2026, with Johor expected to contribute a few thousand additional units following earlier deployments, while Kuala Lumpur City Hall is also engaging the group for a potential next phase of 5,000 to 10,000 units as part of its longer term target of 20,000 units by 2028.

Beyond domestic opportunities, Hong Leong Investment Bank noted that Kota Kinabalu has also called for CCTV tenders which ITMAX intends to participate in, reinforcing expectations of a broader national rollout trajectory driven by smart city initiatives and digitalisation demand across local authorities.

As of 11.02 am, the stock price dips 0.61% to RM4.91.

data.title: Bursa Rebounds To Close Higher As US-Iran De-Escalation Hopes Lift Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia staged a late-session recovery to close higher on April 16, as improving global sentiment, driven by renewed talks between the US and Iran, helped steady investor confidence.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 6.29 points, or 0.37%, to 1,689.71, reversing earlier losses after dipping to an intraday low of 1,682.81.

The rebound was mirrored across broader indices, with the FBM 70 climbing 138.40 points (0.79%) to 17,641.67, while the FBM Emas and FBM Shariah indices advanced 62.36 points (0.5%) and 74.77 points (0.61%) respectively, signalling a broad-based market recovery.

Trading activity picked up notably, with total turnover rising to 3.77 billion shares valued at RM3.46 billion compared to 3.33 billion shares worth RM3.02 billion in the previous session.

Among the most actively traded counters, Borneo Oil Bhd dominated volumes with more than 182.3 million shares, while Zetrix AI Bhd extended gains amid sustained retail interest. MMAG Holdings Bhd and VS Industry Bhd also featured prominently in active trading.

The upbeat close reflects a market increasingly sensitive to geopolitical signals, with easing tensions in West Asia helping to stabilise risk appetite after recent volatility.

While uncertainties remain, the day’s turnaround suggests investors are cautiously positioning for a more stable near-term outlook, supported by improving external cues and sustained domestic participation.

data.title: Stocks Reverse As Investors Await News On US-Iran Peace Talks
data.description:
Stock markets fell on Friday as investors awaited news of an extension to the Iran-US ceasefire, while crude prices edged back down following the previous day’s rally.

The losses follow a healthy, record-breaking week for equities fuelled by hopes the Middle East war, which is heading into a seventh week, could be close to an end after Donald Trump said negotiators were close to a deal.

But worries abound that a shaky truce agreed earlier this month — and which ends next week — could fall apart and spark a fresh market rout.

The US president on Thursday struck an optimistic tone, telling reporters that “it’s looking very good that we’re going to make a deal with Iran, and it’s going to be a good deal”, adding that talks between Washington and Tehran could resume this weekend.

He also claimed Iran had “agreed to give us back the nuclear dust”, using his name for the country’s enriched uranium stockpile, and the deal would include “free oil” as well as the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

“We had to make sure that Iran never gets a nuclear weapon,” Trump said at the White House. “They’ve totally agreed to that. They’ve agreed to almost everything, so maybe if they can get to the table, there’s a difference.”

Iran has given no public indication it would surrender its stockpile.

However, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth took a tough line on the situation earlier in the day, telling a Pentagon news conference: “If Iran chooses poorly, then they will have a blockade and bombs dropping on infrastructure, power and energy.”

Meanwhile, some Gulf Arab and European leaders fear a long-term agreement could take six months to achieve and called for the truce to cover such a time period, Bloomberg reported.

They wanted the Strait of Hormuz — through which about a fifth of global oil and LNG passes — opened immediately and have warned in private of a global food crisis if that is not achieved by next month, the report said.

Stocks fell across the region, with Tokyo, which hit a record high Thursday, among the biggest losers, with Seoul, Hong Kong, Shanghai and Singapore also well down.

Taiwan’s TAIEX fell. On Thursday it hit a market capitalisation of US$4.14 trillion to top the UK’s market capitalisation and become the world’s seventh biggest, according to Bloomberg data.

That came even after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq enjoyed record closes on Wall Street.

Analysts said traders were heading into the weekend to positioning for any surprise developments.

Oil prices dropped more than one percent, a day after sharp gains, though both main contracts remain just below $100 a barrel.

There was some support from a 10-day ceasefire agreed between Israel and Lebanon that took effect at 2100 GMT Thursday.

Tel Aviv has sent troops into its northern neighbour since militant group Hezbollah launched rocket attacks in support of Iran last month.

Hezbollah has not officially said if it will recognise the ceasefire but one of its lawmakers told AFP on Thursday that the group would respect it if Israeli attacks on its militants stopped.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon offered an opportunity for a “historic peace agreement”, but insisted that the disarmament of militant group Hezbollah remained a precondition.

Trump said he will invite the countries’ leaders to the White House.

“While investors remain buoyed by talks of an extension in the US-Iran ceasefire and an announced Israel-Lebanon 10 day ceasefire, risk sentiment remains fragile as an immediate deal remains unlikely given that the countries remain far apart on key issues,” wrote National Australia Bank’s Skye Masters.

Fiona Cincotta of City Index, said: “While risks remain — particularly around disruptions to key shipping routes such as the Strait of Hormuz — markets are increasingly pricing in a scenario where oil prices have peaked unless tensions re-escalate.”

But she warned “the outlook remains fragile. A breakdown in diplomacy or renewed escalation could quickly reverse recent gains”.

data.title: Tokyo Record Leads Asia Stocks Higher As Iran Peace Hopes Grow
data.description:
Japanese stocks hit a record high as Asian equities extended the week’s rally Thursday on heightened optimism the United States and Iran will extend their ceasefire for further talks to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

The gains tracked Wall Street, which also saw all-time peaks as investors also cheered by healthy earnings that indicated the US economy remained resilient despite surging oil prices and rising inflation.

With the Middle East crisis approaching its seventh week, officials from Washington and Tehran were said to be set for a second round of peace talks in Islamabad.

However, that came as Iran also threatened to shut down the Red Sea, along with the Gulf and Sea of Oman, unless the United States lifted a naval blockade of its ports put in place by President Donald Trump following the failure of negotiations last weekend.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters further talks “would very likely” be in the Pakistani capital. “Those discussions are being had,” said, adding that “we feel good about the prospects of a deal”.

US Vice President JD Vance, who led the first round of talks, said Iran is being offered a “grand bargain” to end the conflict.

A Pakistani delegation arrived in Tehran with a new message from Washington after Trump indicated negotiations could resume this week.

A spokesman at Iran’s foreign ministry said “several messages” had been exchanged via Islamabad since talks finished Sunday.

That came as IMF boss Kristalina Georgieva warned of “tough times ahead” for the global economy if the war is not resolved and oil prices remain elevated, adding that inflation risks could seep into food prices.

Still, data Thursday showed China’s economy, the world’s second-biggest, grew a forecast-beating 5.0 percent in the first three months of the year.

US investors welcomed the news with open arms, with the S&P 500 ending above 7,000 points for the first time and the Nasdaq closing higher than 24,000 for the first time.

Tokyo and Seoul again led the rally in Asia, with Japan’s Nikkei hitting a fresh record, as traders poured back into the AI-based tech investments that had helped send the markets surging before war broke out on February 28.

Hong Kong, Shanghai, Singapore, Sydney, Taipei and Manila also pushed higher.

Oil prices were flat but held well below $100 a barrel as traders await the reopening of the key Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of oil and gas passes and has been effectively shut by Iran.

“The markets have already shaken hands, even though the diplomats are still arguing over the wording of the peace treaty,” wrote Stephen Innes at SPI Asset Management.

“The shift is profound. What began as hope has hardened into something far more constructive and far more directional.

“Hope has given way to a bright, beaming light at the end of the peace tunnel. The market is no longer asking whether there will be a deal. It is trading as if the deal is already signed, sealed, and quietly filed away.”

The upbeat mood has also been helped by healthy earnings from large US banks that attested to resilience among US businesses and customers in the face of the Middle East crisis.

There appeared little major reaction to Trump’s renewed threat to sack Federal Reserve boss Jerome Powell if he stays beyond his mandate on May 15.

The central banker said last month he would not leave his post as a Fed governor until a Justice Department investigation involving him is “well and truly over, with transparency and finality”.

data.title: Oil Crisis Weighs In On Malaysia’s Property Sector
data.description:
MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research) has downgraded the property sector to NEUTRAL from Positive, while maintaining Buy calls on Mah Sing Group with a lower target price of RM1.25 from RM1.49 and Matrix Concepts with a revised target price of RM1.55 from RM1.66.

The research house cites weakening loan demand, rising construction costs and softer buying sentiment amid inflationary pressures and higher fuel prices.

The research house said Bank Negara Malaysia data showed property loan applications fell to RM36.6 billion in February 2026, down 22.5% year on year and marking the first annual decline in seven months, while approved loans dropped to RM15.7 billion, down 11.6% year on year as softer applications outweighed a higher approval ratio.

On a cumulative basis, total loan applications for 2MCY26 stood at RM89.7 billion, down 2.9% year on year, while approved loans rose 3.6% year on year to RM37.9 billion, supported by improved approval ratios, although MBSB Investment Bank expects momentum to remain subdued going forward.

MBSB Research said rising oil prices and higher diesel costs are increasing construction expenses, which typically account for about half of developers’ total costs. It added that while ongoing projects are largely insulated due to locked-in contracts, new launches are expected to face higher input costs, with limited ability for developers to pass through price increases due to weaker demand conditions.

MBSB Research estimated that a 10% rise in construction costs could lead to a 300 to 400 basis point compression in gross profit margins, while also warning that higher inflation and reduced disposable income could weaken property sales by 10% to 15% as buyers delay big ticket purchases.

It further highlighted that reductions in the BUDI95 monthly fuel quota to 200 litres from 300 litres are expected to raise commuting costs for suburban households, potentially dampening demand for township developments located outside Kuala Lumpur.

Against this backdrop, the research house said developers with higher exposure to property development earnings such as Mah Sing Group and Matrix Concepts are more vulnerable to near term demand weakness, while those with stronger recurring income streams may be more resilient, as it maintained a more cautious outlook for the sector amid rising property overhang levels.

data.title: Gainers Dominate Stock Movement As MPI, F&N Lead Market Advance
data.description:
The market movement extended its positive momentum, with gainers decisively outpacing losers 753 to 486, reflecting improving investor sentiment and broad-based buying interest across key sectors.

Leading the charge was Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI), which climbed 98 sen to RM33.98, followed by Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, up 58 sen to RM30.68. Plantation heavyweight United Plantations Bhd also advanced 56 sen to RM33.56.

Technology-linked UMS Integration Ltd gained 40 sen to RM5.90, while Life Water Bhd surged 34 sen to RM1.48 on heavy trading volume, making it one of the most actively traded gainers.

On the downside, plantation counters weighed on the market, led by Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd, which fell 42 sen to RM21.08, and PPB Group Bhd, down 18 sen to RM11.54.

Other notable decliners included Gas Malaysia Bhd, Ajinomoto Malaysia Bhd and KLCC Property Holdings Bhd, reflecting pockets of profit-taking in utilities and consumer-related names.

A total of 531 counters were unchanged, while 961 remained untraded and 23 suspended, underscoring selective participation in the market.

The session highlights a market tilt towards selective accumulation, with investors rotating into industrials and technology plays while trimming exposure in plantation and defensive counters.
